title: identity — HTTP middleware
description: Two middleware. Both are AUTH-INFRASTRUCTURE middleware — mounted by the `identity/auth` module on the login + password-reset + MFA-challenge endpoints (which this module does not directly own). They are declared here because they enforce Identity-row-level rules (lockout state, per-IP login velocity) that only the `identity` module has ownership of.

middleware.description: Aggressive per-IP + per-email rate limit on login attempts. 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP; 10 attempts per hour per email. Uses laravel/framework's RateLimiter with a separate bucket per (ip, email). Returns 429 with Retry-After header on exceed. Runs BEFORE Sanctum's guard resolution so authenticated users are still bounded.

Resolves the Identity from the request email + rejects with 423 (Locked) if `locked_until > now()`. Response body includes the unlock timestamp so the SPA can render a countdown. Runs AFTER `identity.rate_limit_login` (which bounds by request velocity) but BEFORE the password-check action (so a locked identity never has its hash compared — timing-consistent regardless of lockout state).

consumed.notes: Global request-throttle applies alongside; the identity rate limits are ON TOP for the sensitive endpoints.

body.message: Too many login attempts. Try again later.
Account is locked. Reset your password to unlock.
